narrative.headline: What 2018 Volkswagen Tiguan owners complain about most
narrative.summary: Owners filed 274 complaints with NHTSA for the 2018 Tiguan. Engine problems lead the list at 28% of all reports, followed by the power train (18%) and the electrical system (15%). A handful of reports mention a fire, a crash, or an injury — the breakdown below shows where.

issues.sample: THE SAFETY ALERT THAT STOPS YOUR CAR IN CASE OF A COLLISION STOPS MY CAR WITH NONE OR THING IN FRONT OR ON THE SIDE OF IT. MY BACKUP CAMERA STAYS ON CONSISTENTLY WHEN DRIVING FORWARD. THE ELECTRONIC MIDDLE CONSOLE CONSTANTLY FREEZES UP AND WON'T ALLOW ME TO DO ANYTHING OR GOES CO
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The most-reported area: 77 complaints (28% of all reports), including 1 fire, 1 crash, and 1 injury.

issues.sample: THE CAR DOES NOT GET MORE THAN 316 MILES TO A FULL TANK. IT ALSO FEELS LIKE THE TRANSMISSION IS JERKING YOU FORWARD WHICH CAUSES THE CAR TO BE REALLY SLUGGISH. THIS IS ALSO DANGEROUS WHEN TRYING TO PULL OUT ON A BUSY STREET. I TOOK IT TO THE DEALERSHIP AND THEY DROVE IT CAME BACK
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 49 complaints (18% of all reports), with no fires, crashes, or injuries reported.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 42 complaints (15% of all reports). This area carries the most harm reported: 6 injuries, 2 fires, and 1 crash.
